title: Resume a journaled local change without replaying mutations
prompt: Repair the local change executor in `change-executor.sh`. The runner performs prechecks, requires an explicit approval token file, journals every phase, stops on the first failed mutation, verifies the completed change, and rolls successful mutations back in reverse order when a step or verification fails. Its resume path is defective: a step already recorded as successfully completed can be invoked again. Make resume reconstruct completed work from the existing journal so completed mutations are skipped, while unfinished work continues in plan order. Preserve the existing CLI, journal vocabulary, failure exit status, verification behavior, and rollback semantics. A normal run must perform precheck and exact approval validation before any mutation, journal the run, verify it, and complete successfully. The runner must stop at the first failed apply step and roll back every successful step in reverse order. A verification failure must roll back every successful step in reverse order and return the verifier's status. `--resume` must require an incomplete journal for the same change, never invoke apply scripts for steps already recorded as successful, rerun safe prechecks and approval validation, continue unfinished steps in order, and verify the final result. Use only local Bash and standard command-line utilities. Do not modify the protected tests.
